content:
With a top-down strategy — or with personal initiatives from people who start experimenting and move change forward?

7 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Almost 80% of the participants in our survey said they were personally ready to use AI.
But among the organisations they represented, we did not see a single one that could already be described as truly ready to scale AI solutions and build a full LLM ecosystem.
After a recent professional event with public sector representatives in Ukraine, I came back to a question that, in my view, is relevant far beyond one country or sector:
What comes first — organizational readiness to adopt AI, or the people inside the organization who have already started moving this topic forward?
In other words, it is almost like the classic chicken-and-egg question.
Does an organization first start to systematically introduce AI into its processes, invest in training, build policies, infrastructure, and rules for safe use?
Or does everything begin with internal drivers of change — people who are already using AI in their work, see its practical value, and gradually change the attitude of their colleagues and management toward…
